content.text:
Implemented locally; not deployed.

- Added authenticated, idempotent `DELETE /static/<path>` returning `204`.
- Renamed `src/lib/static-files*` to [static.ts](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:1) and [static.test.ts](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.test.ts:1).
- Added R2 deletion through the official strongly consistent `bucket.delete()` API. [Cloudflare R2 reference](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/workers/workers-api-reference/)
- Updated [leenk-static](/Users/yusoofsh/.scripts/leenk-static:235) to version `1.1.0`.

Usage after deployment:

```bash
# Interactive confirmation
~/.scripts/leenk-static delete docs/guide.pdf

# Non-interactive
~/.scripts/leenk-static delete --force docs/guide.pdf
```

Authentication uses the same Keychain bearer token as uploads. Invalid credentials return `401` without deleting anything.

`nub run verify` passes: lint, formatting, TypeScript, 16 tests, Wrangler types, and production build. No object was deleted and no production deployment was performed. The unrelated `.node-version` and existing chezmoi drift remain untouched.

I’m applying the autonomy contract first, then the narrow deployment and Git workflow guidance. This audit stays fully read-only and will return an exact production handoff sequence without exposing credentials.

content.text: The key deployment finding is that a push to `main` already triggers the external “Workers Builds: leenk” check. A separate `nub run deploy` would likely create a duplicate deployment, so I’m validating the client’s smoke-test syntax and the check surface before finalizing the sequence.

content.text:
Deployment audit complete and sent to the parent agent.

Current state:

- `e9a5dfc` is committed and ready to push.
- `main` is one commit ahead of `origin/main`.
- Only unrelated `.node-version` drift remains uncommitted.
- Push to `main` automatically triggers CI, CodeQL, and Cloudflare Workers Builds.
- Do not run `nub run deploy` as well; that would duplicate the automatic deployment.
- Safe live proof is homepage `200` plus unauthenticated DELETE returning `401`. A valid-token deletion requires separate approval for the exact path.
